key_insight: A general-purpose coding agent autonomously optimized a crystal graph network for band-gap prediction and achieved the best non-pretrained result on MatBench by combining established crystal and ML techniques.
engineering_relevance: This shows LLM-driven research loops can accelerate materials-model development for structure-to-property prediction benchmarks with clear, fixed evaluation metrics.
method: Built a crystal graph neural network for band-gap prediction
Added element-pair features on message-passing edges
evidence_signal: On the MatBench band-gap benchmark with over 100k crystals, the agent-produced model outperformed all seventeen expert-designed models reported for the task.
watch_item: The gain came from assembling known methods rather than discovering fundamentally new modeling ideas, so autonomous optimization may be limited by reuse of existing techniques.
paper_title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
